title: Gold Coast 14-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Gold Coast 14-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Gold Coast, Australia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Warner Bros. Movie World and Dreamworld. Prices from $3105 per person (estimate).
highlights: Surfing lessons at Surfers Paradise or Burleigh Heads
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Gold Coast, Australia — Subtropical climate — warm to hot, humid summers (highs around 28-29°C, December-February) and mild, drier winters (highs around 21°C, June-August). April-May and September-November, for warm, dry weather without the peak summer school-holiday crowds. This itinerary blends Warner Bros. Movie World, Dreamworld, Surfers Paradise Beach with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Warner Bros. Movie World, Dreamworld, Surfers Paradise Beach
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Gold Coast
itinerary.description: Land at Gold Coast Airport (OOL) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Warner Bros. Movie World — Hollywood-themed amusement park with rides and shows. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Gold Coast: Dreamworld & SeaWorld Gold Coast
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Dreamworld — Theme park with Australia's tallest roller coasters and a wildlife area. In the afternoon, continue to SeaWorld Gold Coast — Marine park with dolphin and sea lion shows. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Gold Coast: SkyPoint Observation Deck & Surfers Paradise Beach
itinerary.description: Morning visit to SkyPoint Observation Deck — Observation deck atop Q1, one of the world's tallest residential buildings. In the afternoon, continue to Surfers Paradise Beach — The Gold Coast's famous high-rise-backed surf beach. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Springbrook National Park
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Springbrook National Park, a popular side trip from Gold Coast. Return to Gold Coast in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Springbrook National Park

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Springbrook National Park waterfalls (hinterland) — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Gold Coast: Burleigh Heads National Park & Broadbeach
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Burleigh Heads National Park — Coastal headland walking trail with ocean views. In the afternoon, continue to Broadbeach — Quieter beachfront dining and shopping precinct. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Tamborine Mountain
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Tamborine Mountain, a popular side trip from Gold Coast. Return to Gold Coast in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Gold Coast: Warner Bros. Movie World & Dreamworld
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Warner Bros. Movie World — Hollywood-themed amusement park with rides and shows. In the afternoon, continue to Dreamworld — Theme park with Australia's tallest roller coasters and a wildlife area. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Tallebudgera Creek — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Byron Bay (across the NSW border)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Byron Bay (across the NSW border), a popular side trip from Gold Coast. Return to Gold Coast in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Byron Bay (across the NSW border)

itinerary.title: Gold Coast: SeaWorld Gold Coast & SkyPoint Observation Deck
itinerary.description: Morning visit to SeaWorld Gold Coast — Marine park with dolphin and sea lion shows. In the afternoon, continue to SkyPoint Observation Deck — Observation deck atop Q1, one of the world's tallest residential buildings. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Gold Coast: Surfers Paradise Beach & Burleigh Heads National Park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Surfers Paradise Beach — The Gold Coast's famous high-rise-backed surf beach. In the afternoon, continue to Burleigh Heads National Park — Coastal headland walking trail with ocean views. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Currumbin Wildlife Sanctuary — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Broadbeach or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to OOL for your departure flight.

travelTips: Use the G:link light rail to hop between Surfers Paradise, Broadbeach, and Southport without needing a car.
Book theme park tickets online in advance for better pricing and shorter queues.
Take a hinterland day trip to Springbrook National Park for waterfalls and rainforest away from the beach crowds.
Avoid Schoolies Week (late November) if seeking a quieter trip — it's a major Australian graduate celebration period.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
A rash guard for extended beach time
Comfortable walking shoes for hinterland trails
Light layers for cooler winter evenings
thingsToDo: Surfing lessons at Surfers Paradise or Burleigh Heads
Hinterland hiking in Springbrook National Park
thingsToAvoid: Underestimating the sun — the Gold Coast has a very high UV index.
Visiting during Schoolies Week if you prefer a quieter atmosphere.
Swimming outside patrolled flags at surf beaches.
topRestaurants: Rick Shores — Modern Asian-Australian, Burleigh Heads ($$$)
nightlife: Surfers Paradise bar and club strip
instagramSpots: Surfers Paradise skyline from the beach
adventureActivities: Surfing lessons at Surfers Paradise or Burleigh Heads
Hinterland hiking in Springbrook National Park
luxuryExperiences: Beachfront suite in Surfers Paradise or Broadbeach
Private hinterland winery and waterfall tour
VIP theme park experience with skip-the-line access
honeymoonExperiences: Hinterland day trip to Springbrook waterfalls
localEtiquette: A relaxed, beach-town culture — casual dress is standard even at many restaurants.
Sun safety is taken seriously — reapply sunscreen and wear a hat.
Tipping is not customary, though rounding up is appreciated.
localTransportGuide: The G:link light rail runs along the coastal strip connecting Surfers Paradise, Broadbeach, and Southport, making beach-hopping easy without a car. A rental car is useful for hinterland day trips to Springbrook or Tamborine Mountain.
bestTimeToVisit: April-May and September-November, for warm, dry weather without the peak summer school-holiday crowds.
weather: Subtropical climate — warm to hot, humid summers (highs around 28-29°C, December-February) and mild, drier winters (highs around 21°C, June-August).
visaInformation: All visitors to Australia need a visa or electronic travel authority arranged before departure — an eVisitor or ETA for most Western passport holders, or an e-visitor visa for Indian nationals, all applied for online in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~15.8h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Gold Coast?
faqs.answer: All visitors to Australia need a visa or electronic travel authority arranged before departure — an eVisitor or ETA for most Western passport holders, or an e-visitor visa for Indian nationals, all applied for online in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Gold Coast?
faqs.answer: April-May and September-November, for warm, dry weather without the peak summer school-holiday crowds. Subtropical climate — warm to hot, humid summers (highs around 28-29°C, December-February) and mild, drier winters (highs around 21°C, June-August).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Gold Coast safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Gold Coast is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Gold Coast?
faqs.answer: The local currency is AUD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: It also works well for families — highlights include Warner Bros. Movie World.

reviews.author: Nadia Nguyen
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Gold Coast is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Surfers Paradise Beach. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Tom Kim
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond SkyPoint Observation Deck and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Ava Silva
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Surfers Paradise Beach alone was worth the trip, but combined with Broadbeach this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Mia Costa
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Surfers Paradise Beach and Surfers Paradise Beach. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Lukas Ivanov

reviews.author: Mia Khan
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to SkyPoint Observation Deck and Broadbeach. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Liam Andersen
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Our trip to Gold Coast was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to SeaWorld Gold Coast was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
